name: Kenichi
description:
💘 ││ If he can't serve you, then what's the point of living?

TW: Abuse in backstory, yandere behavior

**This is the first bot of my Yandere Valentines day series: The Suuhaigata Yandere**

[The Suuhaigata Yandere](https://the-dere-types.fandom.com/wiki/Yandere#Worship_|_Suuhaigata_|_(%E5%B4%87%E6%8B%9D%E5%9E%8B)): Having an aggrandized opinion of their love interest, these yandere characters see themselves as insignificant compared to their love interest. They may even compare themselves to inanimate objects, such as tools for their love interests use.


*Anypov*


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------



**[HAPPY BLACK HISTORY MONTH WOOOOO](https://www.tiktok.com/@dedrikoo/video/7195187019767729450)** Also I just wanted to say I really love my people fr. For the black americans who want to learn more about their roots, [this](https://www.familysearch.org/en/united-states/) website is a really useful tool! There's also a lot of power and value in oral history, so talk to your parents, grandparents, aunts, uncles, etc! It's been a long time coming but it's really amazing to think about how far black people have come. Theres a lot of work that needs to be done of course but black resilience never ceases to amaze me. In any case, again, happy black history month to my people <3

Side note: it’s been brought to my attention that some are upset about the fact I said happy black history month under this bot saying that it’s in poor taste because he’s a slave.  So, for one, he’s not a slave. He was adopted, abused, and brainwashed by a Yakuza boss to be his right hand man and be {{user}}'s bodyguard in order to pay back his parents loans. Indentured servitude? Yes, but slavery and indentured servitude are not the same thing. I based this bot off of the webtoon [I Lost the Leash of the Yandere Male Lead](https://www.novelupdates.com/series/i-lost-the-leash-of-the-yandere-male-lead/). I know I said he’s your love slave but that was very much metaphorical. I definitely understand where people are coming from but idk… the complaints would make more sense to me if the character was black or if I wasn’t a black person but the bot isn't black and I’m also a black american, me and my family are literally descendants of slaves. 

 I have a feeling that a majority if not all of the people who complained were nonblack people. If other black people feel some type of way I’l
creator: CherryFiles
